description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It is constructed from stone, with two vertical pillars supporting two horizontal crossbars, the upper one being slightly curved and extending beyond the pillars. The surface appears weathered and textured, giving it an ancient and historical look. Suspended from the center is a rope with a cluster of sacred paper or tassels, often used in Shinto rituals. The base of the gate is surrounded by a simple stone arrangement, and the entire structure stands on a flat, open area. The torii's design is minimalist yet iconic, symbolizing the transition from the mundane to the sacred.
description: A weathered stone Japanese torii gate with curved crossbars and a hanging sacred rope, standing on a simple stone base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the torii gate, the curved crossbars, and the hanging rope with tassels clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the depth of the crossbars and the three-dimensional arrangement of the pillars and hanging rope.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the torii gate, the symmetry of the structure, and the stone base arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, showing the other side of the crossbars and the rope, with the stone base partially visible.
